# US-Iran Conflict, Strait of Hormuz Status, Ceasefire Negotiations & Oil Supply Disruption — Research Report (May 2026)

## Executive Summary

The United States and Iran have been in active military conflict since **February 28, 2026**, when the US and Israel launched coordinated strikes ("Operation Midnight Hammer") on Iran's nuclear program, military infrastructure, and government sites. The war has resulted in the **effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz** — the world's most critical oil chokepoint — creating what S&P Global calls **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy."** A Pakistan-mediated ceasefire took effect on April 8 but is fragile and repeatedly violated. Direct US-Iran talks (the first since 1979) failed in April. As of early May 2026, oil prices have roughly doubled from pre-war levels, US gasoline is up ~49%, and the global economy faces significant recession risk.

**Data freshness caveat:** The most detailed data available is current through **May 5, 2026**, with some supplementary data through May 18-20. Events from roughly May 6-21 are not fully covered.

---

## 1. War Origins & Military Situation

- **February 28, 2026:** US and Israel launched "Operation Midnight Hammer" — coordinated strikes on Iran targeting nuclear facilities, military bases, and government sites.
- **Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated** in Israeli airstrikes, reportedly enabled by CIA intelligence. Several family members also killed.
- **Mojtaba Khamenei** (son) selected as new Supreme Leader on March 8-9 by the Assembly of Experts; his authority is not yet consolidated.
- **Over 11,000 targets** struck across Iran as of late March.
- **3,000+ killed** by US-Israeli strikes on Iran; 2,696 killed in Lebanon (Israeli strikes since March 2).
- DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified (March 18) that **Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed**.
- US force posture: **3 Carrier Strike Groups**, **2 Marine Expeditionary Units** (2,200-4,000 Marines), **82nd Airborne Division**, hundreds of combat aircraft.
- War cost: approximately **$25 billion** as of late April 2026.
- No confirmed US ground invasion, though Pentagon has planned ground operations including potential seizure of Kharg Island.

## 2. Strait of Hormuz Status

- The Strait has been **effectively closed to commercial shipping** since February 28.
- Normally carries **~25% of world seaborne oil** and **~20% of global LNG**.
- Tanker traffic dropped **~70% immediately**, then fell to essentially zero.
- **~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners stranded**; vessels from **87 countries** affected.
- **May 4: Trump announced "Project Freedom"** — a naval escort operation to guide stranded ships through the strait, backed by the full US naval task force.
- Maersk confirmed **one US-flagged vessel** successfully exited under military escort.
- **Iran's IRGC** warned ships entering without IRGC coordination "will be stopped by force."
- Iran published an **expanded map of the Strait** extending claims into UAE territorial waters.
- An Iranian lawmaker stated Hormuz **"will never return to the status quo that existed before the war."**
- Maritime expert Alexandru Hudisteanu: *"Any attempt to open the strait will likely be met with resistance from Iran."*

## 3. Ceasefire & Negotiations

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| **Apr 8** | Pakistan-mediated ceasefire takes effect |
| **Apr 11-12** | **Islamabad Talks** — first direct US-Iran talks since 1979; 21 hours; ~300 US delegates (led by VP Vance) vs. ~70 Iranian (led by Ghalibaf). **Talks failed.** |
| **Apr 13** | US imposed **naval blockade** of Iranian ports; CENTCOM turned back 51+ vessels |
| **Apr 20** | US seized Iranian container ship **MV Touska** in Gulf of Oman |
| **Apr 21** | Trump extended ceasefire at Pakistan's request |
| **Apr 22** | Trump gave Iran **3-5 days** to engage in negotiations |
| **May 2** | Iran submitted **14-point ceasefire response**: demands 30-day timeline, full US withdrawal, end of blockade, sanctions lifted, reparations |
| **May 4** | Touska's **22 crew repatriated** to Iran via Pakistan (confidence-building measure) |
| **May 4** | Trump told Axios he wants a deal soon **"or may bomb Iran"** |
| **May 4-5** | **Intense escalation**: Iran attacked UAE oil facilities (Fujairah); US claimed to sink 7 IRGC fast boats; Iran claimed 2 missiles struck a US warship (US denied) |

- Trump described Iran's 14-point proposal as **"unacceptable"** but said negotiations were producing "very positive discussions."
- **Mediating parties:** Pakistan (primary), Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Qatar, Egypt; China involved in communications.
- Ceasefire described as **"on the brink" of collapse** as of May 5.
- Prediction markets: ceasefire probability was ~8% over a 13-day window in late March/early April.

## 4. Oil Supply Disruption & Prices

| Metric | Value | Date/Source |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| Global oil supply loss | ~2 million bpd | IEA, May 2026 |
| Brent crude (pre-war) | ~$70-75/bbl | Late Feb 2026 |
| Brent crude (peak) | >$125/bbl | Late Apr 2026 |
| Brent crude (latest) | ~$109.90/bbl | May 5, 2026 |
| WTI crude | $102.46/bbl | May 5, 2026 |
| ICE Brent July futures (CBN26) | $113.81/bbl settlement | May 4, 2026 |
| Brent 52-week low | $58.68/bbl | Pre-conflict |
| US gasoline (pre-war) | $2.98/gal | Feb 26, 2026 |
| US gasoline (latest) | $4.446/gal (+49%) | May 3, 2026 |
| US SPR releases | 17.5 million barrels | Mar 20 - Apr 24 |
| OPEC+ planned increase | 188,000 bpd | Starting Jun 2026 |
| Med diesel crack spread | ~$62-63/bbl | May 5 (3-6x normal) |
| Venezuela exports (offset) | 1.23 million bpd | Apr 2026 (7-yr high) |
| US imports from Venezuela | 588,000 bpd | Week ending May 8 |

- **Fujairah attack (May 5):** Iran launched 12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, and 4 drones at UAE. Most intercepted; one drone hit the **Fujairah Petroleum Industry Zone** (fire, 3 injured).
- **UAE departed OPEC** amid the crisis.
- **Spirit Airlines shut down**, citing inability to absorb higher fuel costs.

## 5. Macroeconomic & Political Impact

- **US Q1 2026 GDP:** +2.0% annualized; Q2 forecast ~1%.
- **PCE inflation:** +4.5% annualized Q1; 3.5% YoY through March.
- **Euro area energy inflation:** 10.9% (April 2026).
- **Goldman Sachs recession probability:** 30% within 12 months.
- **Trump approval:** Dropped **below 30%**, directly linked to unpopular war.
- **FOMC:** 4 dissents at April meeting (most since 1992); fed funds rate at 3.625%.
- Projections suggest potential **GOP loss of 28 House seats** in 2026 midterms.

## 6. Internal Iranian Dynamics

- **President Pezeshkian** branded IRGC Hormuz escalation as "madness" (May 4) — revealing severe civilian-military tensions.
- Iran is **reconstituting missile and drone capabilities**, reopening bombed underground bases.
- **"Jan Fadaa" mobilization campaign** claims 31 million registrations (critics estimate under 4 million actual).
- Iran's economy in severe crisis: labor market "cracking under layoffs and inflation."
- Near-total **internet shutdown** for 1,550+ hours (10+ weeks), affecting 90 million Iranians.
- Hormuz leverage described as Iran's **"only leverage"** for peace negotiations.

## 7. Key Uncertainties (May 6-21 gap)

The following critical questions remain unanswered due to the data gap:
- Whether "Project Freedom" achieved sustained commercial shipping through Hormuz
- Whether the ceasefire held or formally collapsed
- Whether Trump followed through on his threat to resume bombing
- Current oil price levels (last known: ~$110-114/bbl on May 5)
- Status of Iran's 14-point ceasefire proposal negotiations
- Any new mediating efforts or diplomatic breakthroughs

---

**Sources consulted:** Al Jazeera, AP News, BBC News, Reuters, NPR, CNBC, Washington Post, NBC News, S&P Global, Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, OilPrice.com, Barchart, EIA, IEA, AAA Gas Prices, Iran International, Axios, Fox News, Moody's Analytics, Goldman Sachs, Wikipedia (2026 Iran war), Kalshi, Metaculus, Manifold Markets, Polymarket.

key_facts: The US and Israel launched coordinated military strikes on Iran ('Operation Midnight Hammer') on February 28, 2026, beginning an active armed conflict
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was assassinated in Israeli airstrikes reportedly enabled by CIA intelligence; Mojtaba Khamenei (his son) was selected as new Supreme Leader on March 8-9
The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026 — described by S&P Global as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy'
Approximately 2,000 ships and 20,000 mariners from 87 countries are stranded due to the Hormuz closure
A Pakistan-mediated ceasefire took effect on April 8, 2026, but is described as 'on the brink' of collapse as of early May
The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12) were the first direct US-Iran negotiations since 1979; they lasted 21 hours and failed
The US imposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports on April 13, turning back 51+ commercial vessels
Iran submitted a 14-point ceasefire response on May 2 demanding US withdrawal, end of blockade, sanctions lifting, and reparations — Trump called it 'unacceptable'
Trump announced 'Project Freedom' on May 4 — a naval operation to escort ships through Hormuz backed by 3 Carrier Strike Groups
On May 4-5, hostilities sharply escalated: Iran attacked UAE oil facilities at Fujairah; US claimed to sink 7 IRGC fast boats; Iran claimed to hit a US warship (US denied)
Brent crude peaked above $125/barrel in late April 2026, roughly doubling from pre-war levels of ~$70-75/barrel
US gasoline rose 49% from $2.98/gallon (pre-war) to $4.446/gallon as of May 3, 2026
IEA estimates global oil supply loss of approximately 2 million barrels per day
The US released 17.5 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve between March 20 and April 24
Venezuela oil exports surged to 1.23 million bpd in April 2026 (7-year high), partially offsetting Iranian supply loss
Trump's approval rating dropped below 30%, directly linked to the unpopular war
Goldman Sachs assigned 30% probability of US recession within 12 months
DNI Gabbard testified that Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed by the strikes
Iran's IRGC warned that ships entering Hormuz without IRGC coordination 'will be stopped by force'
Iranian President Pezeshkian branded IRGC Hormuz escalation as 'madness', revealing severe civilian-military tensions within Iran
